summary: Build “Smart Money Vault”, a high-fidelity institutional trading SaaS on ICP with a Dual-Core architecture: an off-chain Python trading bot (decision maker) and an on-chain ICP app that acts as gatekeeper for SaaS licensing and as the immutable trade truth source (Ed25519-verified, stable-memory logged). Frontend is being upgraded to a new “Cupertino Dashboard” (Bloomberg Terminal meets Linear) that replaces the old Phase 1 screens, and includes a Settings “Connect Bot” wizard (browser-generated Ed25519 keys) plus subscription tier UI; all UI must use real backend data (no mock data).
architectural_notes: Tech stack: Motoko backend canister(s); React + TypeScript + Tailwind + Framer Motion frontend; Internet Identity for auth.
Dual-Core system: off-chain Python trading bot (already built) makes decisions; on-chain ICP app is the gatekeeper + truth source.
Gatekeeper responsibility: hold SaaS license state; bot checks canister via HTTP and stops if user not paying.
Truth source responsibility: receive every trade from bot; verify Ed25519 signature and persist trade records immutably.
Modular deployment: separate canisters for Assets (frontend) and Logic (backend).
Scalability requirement: use stable memory for trade logs to allow indefinite growth.
Security requirement: Ed25519 verification for all bot-to-canister calls.
Operations requirement: cycle monitoring and auto-top-up logic.
Frontend UI implemented with shadcn-ui component library on top of Tailwind/Framer Motion for Cupertino Dark styling.
Backend includes a reusable authorization/access-control module/mixin to support admin-only features.
known_issues: Build/process failed to complete PHASE 1 backend canister implementation; user requested retry by splitting into substeps.
Repeated build/process failures when attempting PHASE 1 backend implementation; requires breaking work into even smaller substeps to proceed.
User requests an extremely granular ('atomic') stepwise breakdown because prior Phase 1 backend implementation attempts repeatedly fail; process needs micro-substeps to make progress.
Latest attempt to execute the next backend step (A1: extend UserProfile optional fields) failed in the build system; user requested an even finer micro-step breakdown and retry.

existing_features.title: Modular ICP canister deployment (frontend assets + backend logic)
existing_features.description: Project scaffold includes separate canister configurations for frontend assets and backend logic, with frontend configured to call the backend canister for data operations.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication integrated in frontend app shell
existing_features.description: Frontend includes Internet Identity login flow, persistent signed-in session state, and a protected application shell for authenticated usage.

existing_features.title: Cupertino Dark frontend UI foundation with core screens (Overview, Trades, Admin)
existing_features.description: Frontend implements the institutional Cupertino Dark UI foundation and core screens: Overview dashboard, Trades ledger view, and an Admin screen, using React + TypeScript + Tailwind + Framer Motion and a shadcn-ui component set.

existing_features.title: Backend authorization/access-control foundation for admin-gated UI and operations
existing_features.description: Backend includes an authorization/access-control layer to support admin-only UI/operations (e.g., Admin screen gating) and general permission checks.

existing_features.title: Create a Phase 1 implementation checklist inside the repo (e.g., a markdown file) that enumerates each backend change as a separately testable step, matching the requirements REQ-69+ below, so work can be executed incrementally without large diffs.

Replace the existing Phase 1 in-app navigation (Overview/Trades/Admin screens) with the Phase 2 “Cupertino Dashboard” as the primary authenticated UI, using the design philosophy “Bloomberg Terminal meets Linear App” and the specified Tailwind styling (background #0A0A0A, cards #121212, borders border-white/5, tracking-tight, text-sm labels, text-3xl font-medium metrics).

Implement the Phase 2 bento-grid layout (CSS Grid with gap-4) with: (1) a minimalist glassmorphism header containing the “Smart Money Vault” logo/title and Internet Identity login/logout control, (2) top-left “System Heartbeat” status card, (3) top-right “Equity Curve” chart panel, and (4) bottom-wide “Live Logs” audit-feed table panel.

Implement the Phase 2 “System Heartbeat” card UI with a pulsing indicator and real backend wiring: show bot status with a green/red dot, license status, and cycle health status using the new backend status API.

Implement the Phase 2 “Equity Curve” panel using Recharts with the requested styling (monotone green line, gradient fill below, no grid lines) and wire it to the backend equity curve API (no mock data).

Implement the Phase 2 “Live Logs” audit-feed table with fixed-width fonts for numeric fields and Buy/Sell color semantics (Buy in green, Sell in red), and wire it to the backend audit/logs API with pagination/refresh behavior appropriate for a “live” feed.
Add Framer Motion entry animations to the Phase 2 dashboard panels and key UI elements, keeping motion subtle and consistent with the “Bloomberg Terminal meets Linear App” design direction.
Update the unauthenticated landing experience to be a minimalist “Welcome to the Vault” screen with centered Internet Identity login, while preserving the existing authentication flow behavior.

Create a new Settings page that includes a “Connect Bot” workflow for browser-side Ed25519 keypair generation: provide a “Generate Bot Identity” button, display the private key exactly once in a blurred field with a Copy button, and show the warning text: “Paste this into your Python Bot's .env file as BOT_PRIVATE_KEY.”
Wire the Settings “Connect Bot” workflow to call the new backend API to persist/link the generated public key to the user’s Principal, and provide clear success/error UI states (no mock outcomes).

Add a “Subscription” tab in Settings that displays three glassmorphism tier cards: “Paper Hand” (Free): View-only, delayed data; “Diamond Hand” ($50/mo): Live Trading, SaaS License Active; “Whale” ($500/mo): Priority execution, Concierge support.

Use `lucide-react` for icons across the new Dashboard and Settings UI, and adopt `clsx` + `tailwind-merge` for className composition in newly created components (without modifying read-only UI component sources).

new_feature_requests.summary: Implement Dual-Core integration: Python trading bot calls ICP canister endpoints for license checks (gatekeeper) and trade submissions (truth source).
SaaS license gatekeeper canister/API: store subscription/license state and expose an HTTP-accessible endpoint for bot to verify active payment before trading.
Verify Ed25519 signatures for all bot-to-canister calls before accepting license checks/trade logs.
Immutable trade logging to stable memory to support effectively unbounded scaling of trade history.
Cycle monitoring and auto-top-up logic for self-optimizing canister operations.

Create the backend canister APIs for the Dual-Core integration: (1) a license check endpoint intended for the off-chain Python trading bot (Gatekeeper), and (2) a trade submission endpoint intended for the off-chain Python trading bot (Truth Source). Both endpoints must be callable without Internet Identity (bot-to-canister flow) and must return clear machine-readable responses.

Verify Ed25519 signatures for all bot-to-canister calls (at minimum: license checks and trade submissions) using a configured bot public key stored on-chain, with replay protection (nonce and/or timestamp window) to prevent replays of signed messages.

Implement SaaS license state in the backend canister: store license/subscription status per user (or account identifier) and expose authenticated (Internet Identity) admin operations to create/update/revoke licenses and to view license state.

Persist an append-only, immutable trade ledger in stable memory (stable structures) that supports effectively unbounded growth via chunking/pagination, and expose query APIs to retrieve trade history with cursor-based pagination and filtering by account identifier and time range.

Add backend cycle monitoring: track and expose the canister cycle balance, a configurable warning threshold, and a lightweight status endpoint consumable by the frontend. Include a clear mechanism to accept cycle top-ups (e.g., explicit top-up/accept endpoint) and record a stable audit log of cycle-related events (warnings/top-ups).

Add backend user profile + admin stats API: persist UserProfile (plan tier Free/Pro/Whale, bot_id, Ed25519 public key) and implement get_stats(user_principal) returning total PnL, win rate, and subscription status derived from the immutable trade/audit log.

Update the Motoko backend canister to implement a persistent `users` registry stored in `stable` variables: a map from `Principal` to `UserProfile` containing (1) plan tier (Free/Pro/Whale), (2) `bot_id` (Text), and (3) Ed25519 public key (Blob). Include backend methods necessary to create/update/read this user registry in a secure way (e.g., caller can manage their own profile; admins can manage any user) without breaking existing user-profile functionality used by the current frontend.

Implement a persistent, append-only `audit_logs` data structure stored in `stable` variables that records trade events with fields: Symbol, Price, Side, Timestamp, PnL, ProofHash. The log must be append-only at the API level (no update/delete operations exposed).

Add the Bot Gatekeeper API `validate_bot(bot_id: Text, signature: Blob, timestamp: Int) -> Result<Status, Error>` with replay protection requiring `timestamp` to be within 60 seconds of canister time. The function must (1) locate the owning user for the given `bot_id`, (2) verify `signature` against the stored Ed25519 public key for that user, (3) check whether the user subscription is active (mock for now: default ACTIVE), and (4) return machine-readable `Status` (ACTIVE/SUSPENDED) or an appropriate `Error`.

Add the Audit Log API `log_trade(bot_id: Text, trade_data: TradeData, signature: Blob)` that verifies the Ed25519 signature (using the stored public key for the user owning `bot_id`) before appending `trade_data` to the stable, append-only `audit_logs`. Reject (trap or return error, as appropriate for the chosen API shape) any call with invalid signature or unknown bot_id.

Implement `get_stats(user_principal: Principal)` that returns total PnL, win rate, and subscription status for the specified user. Stats must be derived from the immutable `audit_logs` (e.g., filtering the log entries attributable to that user via the user’s stored bot_id). Enforce appropriate access control (at minimum: admin-only for arbitrary principals, while a user can request their own stats).

Make the new `users` and `audit_logs` persistence production-ready with canister upgrade safety: use `stable` variables and implement any necessary `preupgrade`/`postupgrade` logic so state is preserved across upgrades in accordance with the project’s single-actor backend architecture.
Ensure the backend canister is named `vault_backend` in the project configuration (canister definition) so the production Motoko backend canister is deployed under that name.

Update the Motoko backend to persist all critical backend state across upgrades using `stable` variables plus `system preupgrade`/`system postupgrade` logic, without breaking existing public APIs currently used by the frontend (e.g., user profile, license management, trade retrieval).

Implement a persistent `users` registry stored in stable memory as a map from `Principal` to an extended `UserProfile` that includes: plan tier (Free/Pro/Whale), `bot_id` (Text), and Ed25519 public key (Blob), while preserving the existing frontend-required profile fields (`name`, `accountId`).

Add backend helper indexing to resolve `bot_id` to the owning user principal efficiently (e.g., maintain a `bot_id -> Principal` index in stable memory and keep it consistent when profiles are created/updated).

Implement the Bot Gatekeeper API `validate_bot(bot_id: Text, signature: Blob, timestamp: Int) -> Result<Status, Error>` with replay protection requiring `timestamp` within 60 seconds of canister time, verifying the signature against the owning user’s stored Ed25519 public key, and returning machine-readable `Status` (ACTIVE/SUSPENDED) or `Error`. Subscription check must be mocked for now, defaulting to ACTIVE when the user exists and signature/time checks pass.

Implement a persistent, append-only `audit_logs` data structure in stable memory that records trade events with fields: Symbol, Price, Side, Timestamp, PnL, ProofHash. Expose it only through append/read APIs (no update/delete operations).

Implement the Audit Log API `log_trade(bot_id: Text, trade_data: TradeData, signature: Blob)` that verifies the Ed25519 signature using the owning user’s stored public key (resolved via `bot_id`) before appending `trade_data` to `audit_logs`. Reject any call with invalid signature or unknown `bot_id` with a machine-readable error (or a consistent trap strategy, but it must be documented in candid/types).

Implement `get_stats(user_principal: Principal)` returning total PnL, win rate, and subscription status for the specified user, derived from the immutable `audit_logs` attributable to that user (via the user’s `bot_id`). Enforce access control: a user can request their own stats; admin can request stats for any principal.
Ensure the backend canister is deployed under the name `vault_backend` in the project’s canister configuration, while keeping the existing single-actor backend architecture intact.
Add/extend backend domain types to support Phase 1 without breaking existing candid APIs used by the current frontend (user profiles, license management, trade retrieval).

Implement a new persistent `users` registry as a stable map from `Principal` to an extended user profile that includes: (1) existing fields `name : Text` and `accountId : ?Text`, plus (2) `planTier : {#Free; #Pro; #Whale}`, (3) `bot_id : ?Text`, and (4) `botPublicKey : ?Blob` (Ed25519 public key bytes).
Add a stable index `bot_id -> Principal` and keep it consistent with the `users` registry whenever a user (or admin) sets/changes/removes a bot_id.

Add backend APIs to manage the extended `users` registry securely: (1) caller can read/update their own extended profile; (2) admin can read/update any user’s extended profile; and (3) bot key and bot_id can be managed per-user (not as a single global key).

Replace the current placeholder bot signature verification with real Ed25519 verification against the per-user stored public key, and define a canonical message format for signatures used by `validate_bot` and `log_trade` (documented in candid comments).
Implement replay protection for bot-to-canister calls using a strict timestamp window of 60 seconds for `validate_bot` (and apply equivalent replay protection to `log_trade`).

Add the Bot Gatekeeper API `validate_bot(bot_id: Text, signature: Blob, timestamp: Int) -> Result<Status, Error>` that: (1) resolves bot_id to owning user; (2) enforces the 60s timestamp window; (3) verifies the Ed25519 signature using the owning user’s stored public key; (4) checks subscription status (mock for now: default ACTIVE when user exists and checks pass); and (5) returns `#ok(#ACTIVE|#SUSPENDED)` or `#err(Error)` with clear error cases.

Create a persistent, append-only `audit_logs` structure in stable memory that records immutable trade events with fields: `symbol : Text`, `price : Float`, `side : {#buy; #sell}`, `timestamp : Int`, `pnl : Float`, `proofHash : Blob`, and also stores enough linkage to attribute an event to a user via `bot_id` (directly or via stored bot_id at log time).

Add the Audit Log API `log_trade(bot_id: Text, trade_data: TradeData, signature: Blob)` that: (1) resolves bot_id to owning user; (2) verifies the Ed25519 signature over a canonical encoding of `(bot_id, trade_data)`; and (3) appends the trade event to `audit_logs` if valid; otherwise returns a machine-readable error (do not trap for expected errors).

Implement `get_stats(user_principal: Principal)` returning total PnL, win rate, and subscription status derived from the immutable `audit_logs`, attributing events to the specified user via that user’s bot_id (or stored attribution field). Enforce access control: a user can request their own stats; admins can request stats for any principal.

Add stable upgrade safety for all new critical state (`users`, `bot_id` index, `audit_logs`, and replay-protection state) using `stable` variables and `system preupgrade`/`system postupgrade`, while preserving existing state behavior for licenses, trades, and profiles.
Ensure the backend canister is deployed under the name `vault_backend` in the project canister configuration, without changing the single-actor backend architecture.

Inventory the existing backend public APIs currently used by the frontend (user profile, license management, trade retrieval, admin bot key) and ensure all of them continue to exist with backward-compatible candid types after Phase 1 changes.

Extend the backend domain types to support Phase 1 without breaking existing profile functionality: update UserProfile to retain existing fields `name : Text` and `accountId : ?Text` and add optional Phase 1 fields `planTier : {#Free; #Pro; #Whale}`, `bot_id : ?Text`, and `botPublicKey : ?Blob` (Ed25519 public key bytes).
Implement a persistent `users` registry stored in stable memory mapping `Principal -> UserProfile` (extended per REQ-38), and route existing profile reads/writes through this single source of truth.
Add a stable, consistent index to resolve `bot_id -> Principal` and keep it correct whenever a user or admin sets/changes/removes a bot_id in the user profile.

Add secure backend APIs to manage the extended user registry: (1) caller can read/update their own extended profile; (2) admin can read/update any user’s extended profile; (3) bot_id and botPublicKey are stored per-user (not a single global key).

Define and document (in Motoko comments near the type/API definitions) a canonical message format used for Ed25519 signatures for `validate_bot` and `log_trade`, including how `bot_id`, timestamps, and trade data are serialized into bytes prior to verification.

Replace the current placeholder bot signature verification with real Ed25519 verification against the per-user stored public key, and ensure all bot-to-canister calls that accept `signature` reject invalid signatures with a machine-readable error (do not trap for expected failures).

Implement replay protection using a strict timestamp window of 60 seconds for `validate_bot` and apply equivalent replay protection to `log_trade` (timestamp window and/or nonce tracking), using stable state so replays remain blocked across upgrades.

Implement the Bot Gatekeeper API `validate_bot(bot_id: Text, signature: Blob, timestamp: Int) -> Result<Status, Error>` that: (1) resolves bot_id to owning user via the stable index; (2) enforces 60-second timestamp window; (3) verifies Ed25519 signature using that user’s stored public key; (4) checks subscription status (mock for now: default ACTIVE when user exists and checks pass); and (5) returns machine-readable `Status` (#ACTIVE/#SUSPENDED) or `Error` via Result.

Create a persistent, append-only `audit_logs` structure in stable memory that records immutable trade events with fields: `symbol : Text`, `price : Float`, `side : {#buy; #sell}`, `timestamp : Int`, `pnl : Float`, `proofHash : Blob`, and stores enough linkage to attribute the event to a user (e.g., store `bot_id` or owner principal at log time).

Implement the Audit Log API `log_trade(bot_id: Text, trade_data: TradeData, signature: Blob)` that: (1) resolves bot_id to owning user; (2) verifies the Ed25519 signature over the canonical message encoding; (3) enforces replay protection; and (4) appends the trade event to the stable append-only `audit_logs` when valid, otherwise returns a machine-readable error.

Implement `get_stats(user_principal: Principal)` returning total PnL, win rate, and subscription status derived from the immutable `audit_logs` attributable to that user (via bot_id or stored attribution), with access control: a user can request their own stats; admins can request stats for any principal.

Make all new critical backend state upgrade-safe using stable variables and `system preupgrade`/`system postupgrade` so state is preserved across upgrades for: users registry, bot_id index, audit_logs, and replay-protection state.
Ensure the backend canister is deployed under the name `vault_backend` in the project canister configuration, without changing the single-actor backend architecture.

Inventory current backend public APIs and types in `backend/main.mo` and define a backward-compatibility plan so existing frontend flows (profile save/read, license management, trades pagination, admin bot key management) continue to work after Phase 1 changes.

Extend the `UserProfile` type to preserve existing fields (`name`, `accountId`) and add optional Phase 1 fields: `planTier : {#Free; #Pro; #Whale}`, `bot_id : ?Text`, and `botPublicKey : ?Blob` (Ed25519 public key bytes).

Replace the current in-memory `userProfiles` map with a single source-of-truth `users` registry keyed by `Principal` that stores the extended `UserProfile`, while preserving the semantics of existing profile read/write methods.
Introduce a stable index `bot_id -> Principal` and keep it consistent whenever a user (self) or admin updates a user’s `bot_id` field.

Add admin-capable APIs to read/update any user’s extended profile (including `planTier`, `bot_id`, `botPublicKey`) while preserving the existing rule that normal users can only edit their own profile.

Define Phase 1 domain types for `TradeData`, `AuditTradeEvent`, `Status`, and `Error`, using machine-readable variants for success/error and avoiding traps for expected failures (unknown bot_id, invalid signature, timestamp window violation).

Define and document (in Motoko comments adjacent to the new API/type definitions) a canonical message format for Ed25519 signatures used by `validate_bot` and `log_trade`, including how `bot_id`, `timestamp`, and trade fields are serialized into bytes prior to verification.
Replace the current placeholder `verifyBotSignature` implementation with real Ed25519 signature verification against the per-user stored public key (resolved via `bot_id`).

Implement replay protection for bot-to-canister calls using a strict 60-second timestamp window for `validate_bot` and apply equivalent replay protection to `log_trade` (timestamp window and/or stable nonce tracking), with state persisted in stable memory so it remains effective across upgrades.

Create a persistent, append-only `audit_logs` structure in stable memory to record immutable trade events with fields: `symbol`, `price`, `side`, `timestamp`, `pnl`, `proofHash`, plus an attribution field sufficient to link events to the owning user (e.g., store `bot_id` and/or owner principal at log time).

Add the Bot Gatekeeper API `validate_bot(bot_id: Text, signature: Blob, timestamp: Int) -> Result<Status, Error>` that resolves `bot_id` to the owning user, enforces the 60-second timestamp window, verifies the Ed25519 signature using that user’s stored public key, mocks subscription check as ACTIVE by default, and returns `#ok(#ACTIVE|#SUSPENDED)` or `#err(Error)`.

Add the Audit Log API `log_trade(bot_id: Text, trade_data: TradeData, signature: Blob) -> Result<(), Error>` that resolves `bot_id`, verifies Ed25519 signature over the canonical encoding of `(bot_id, trade_data, timestamp/replay fields)`, enforces replay protection, and appends the immutable event to `audit_logs` only when valid.

Implement a query API to read `audit_logs` with pagination (cursor or start/limit) and optional filtering by time range and/or bot_id, to support scalable retrieval without returning unbounded arrays.

Implement `get_stats(user_principal: Principal)` returning total PnL, win rate, and subscription status for the specified user, derived from the immutable `audit_logs` attributable to that user, with access control: user can request their own stats; admin can request stats for any principal.

Make Phase 1 persistence upgrade-safe: store all critical state in stable variables and implement `system preupgrade`/`system postupgrade` so `users`, the `bot_id` index, `audit_logs`, and replay-protection state survive upgrades.

Preserve existing license and trade APIs while integrating Phase 1 changes safely: ensure current `createOrUpdateLicense`, `revokeLicense`, `getAllLicenses`, `getMyLicenseStatus`, and `getTradesPaginated` continue to compile and behave, even if Phase 1 introduces new bot-facing endpoints (`validate_bot`, `log_trade`).
Ensure the backend canister is deployed under the name `vault_backend` in the project canister configuration (e.g., `dfx.json`), without changing the single-actor architecture.

Inventory and document (in comments near the API/type section in backend/main.mo) all current public methods and types that the existing frontend depends on, and explicitly mark them as “must remain backward compatible”.

Extend the backend UserProfile type in backend/main.mo by adding OPTIONAL Phase 1 fields while preserving existing fields exactly: keep `name : Text` and `accountId : ?Text`, and add `planTier : ?{#Free; #Pro; #Whale}`, `bot_id : ?Text`, and `botPublicKey : ?Blob`.

Introduce new backend domain types (in backend/main.mo) needed for bot endpoints, each as minimal standalone additions: (1) `Status` variant with `#ACTIVE` and `#SUSPENDED`; (2) `Error` variant with machine-readable cases for at least: `#Unauthorized`, `#UnknownBotId`, `#MissingBotKey`, `#InvalidSignature`, `#TimestampOutOfWindow`, `#ReplayDetected`, `#LicenseInactive`, `#InvalidInput`; (3) a Result alias or explicit `variant { #ok : T; #err : Error }` shape for bot endpoints.

Add Phase 1 trade logging types in backend/main.mo as minimal additions: define `TradeData` (symbol, price, side, timestamp, pnl, proofHash) and define an immutable stored `AuditTradeEvent` that includes enough attribution to link events to an owner (store at least `bot_id` or owner `Principal` at log time).

Replace the current single global `botPublicKey : ?Blob` design with per-user storage by introducing a new persistent “users registry” abstraction in code: define the intent that the source of truth is `Principal -> UserProfile` (extended), and ensure all profile reads/writes flow through that single source of truth.
Introduce a stable index `bot_id -> Principal` in backend/main.mo and define invariants (in code comments) for keeping it consistent with the users registry whenever `bot_id` is set/changed/removed.

Add a small internal helper in backend/main.mo to resolve a `bot_id : Text` to an owning Principal using the stable index, returning a machine-readable Error when not found (do not trap for expected failures).

Add secure backend APIs to manage the extended per-user fields without changing existing frontend flows: (1) caller can update their own `planTier`, `bot_id`, and `botPublicKey`; (2) admin can update these fields for any Principal; and (3) updates keep `bot_id -> Principal` index consistent.

Define a canonical message format for Ed25519 signatures and document it in backend/main.mo comments adjacent to the new bot APIs/types, including exactly how `bot_id`, `timestamp`, and (for trade logging) trade fields are serialized to bytes prior to verification.

Replace the placeholder `verifyBotSignature` behavior with real Ed25519 verification against the per-user stored public key (resolved by `bot_id`), and ensure the verification helper returns machine-readable errors instead of trapping for expected failures.

Implement replay protection state as stable data: add stable structures needed to enforce replay protection across upgrades, and define a minimal strategy (timestamp window and/or nonce tracking) that can be reused by both `validate_bot` and `log_trade`.

Implement strict timestamp window validation of 60 seconds for bot-to-canister calls as a standalone helper in backend/main.mo (using canister time) that returns `#err(#TimestampOutOfWindow)` when violated.

Add the new bot-facing Gatekeeper API `validate_bot(bot_id: Text, signature: Blob, timestamp: Int) -> Result<Status, Error>` in backend/main.mo that performs its checks in explicitly ordered micro-steps: (1) resolve bot_id -> owner; (2) enforce 60s timestamp window; (3) verify signature over the canonical message; (4) check subscription status (mock: ACTIVE by default when user exists and checks pass); (5) return `#ok(#ACTIVE|#SUSPENDED)` or `#err(Error)`.

Introduce a new persistent, append-only `audit_logs` structure stored in stable memory, with a minimal internal API: `append(event)` and `readPage(start, limit)` (or cursor equivalent), and no update/delete capability exposed.
Add a query API to read audit logs with pagination and basic filtering (time range and/or bot_id) that does not return unbounded arrays.

Add the bot-facing Audit Log API `log_trade(bot_id: Text, trade_data: TradeData, signature: Blob) -> Result<(), Error>` in backend/main.mo with explicit micro-steps: (1) resolve bot_id -> owner; (2) enforce replay protection (timestamp window and/or nonce strategy, persisted); (3) verify signature over canonical encoding of `(bot_id, trade_data, replay fields)`; (4) append an AuditTradeEvent to `audit_logs` with attribution; (5) return `#ok(())` or `#err(Error)` (no traps for expected errors).

Implement `get_stats(user_principal: Principal)` in backend/main.mo as a standalone feature with strict access control: the caller can request their own stats; admins can request stats for any principal; others receive a machine-readable `#err(#Unauthorized)` (or equivalent).

Compute `get_stats` results strictly from immutable `audit_logs` by filtering events attributable to the target user (via stored attribution field and/or the user’s bot_id), and return at minimum: total PnL, win rate, and subscription status.

Make all new Phase 1 critical backend state upgrade-safe using stable variables plus `system preupgrade`/`system postupgrade` in backend/main.mo, covering: users registry, bot_id index, audit_logs, and replay-protection state.

Preserve existing license and trade APIs and behavior while introducing Phase 1 bot endpoints: ensure current methods `createOrUpdateLicense`, `revokeLicense`, `getAllLicenses`, `getMyLicenseStatus`, and `getTradesPaginated` still compile and work, and their candid types remain backward compatible.

Ensure the legacy bot endpoints already present (`getLicenseStatus`, `submitTrade`) are either (a) kept for backward compatibility but clearly marked as legacy and internally routed to the new Phase 1 logic, or (b) replaced with the Phase 1 equivalents in a way that is explicitly documented and does not break existing callers unintentionally.

PHASE 2: Implement the new Cupertino Dashboard UI (Bento grid layout with header + status card + equity curve chart + live audit feed), wired to vault_backend with no mock data, using Tailwind + Framer Motion + Recharts and the specified dark/glassmorphism styling.
PHASE 2 decision: Replace/remove the old Phase 1 frontend screens (Overview, Trades, Admin) so the new Cupertino Dashboard becomes the primary UI.

PHASE 3: Add a Settings page with a “Connect Bot” workflow that generates an Ed25519 keypair in-browser, displays the private key one-time with copy + warning, and links the public key to the user’s Principal by calling a NEW vault_backend API.
PHASE 3: Add Subscription/Tiers UI (Paper Hand Free, Diamond Hand $50/mo, Whale $500/mo) with glassmorphism cards and a Subscription tab in Settings.

Add/extend backend canister query APIs in `backend/main.mo` to support the Dashboard “System Heartbeat” card with real data (no mock data), including fields for: bot status, license status, and canister cycle health (e.g., current cycle balance and/or a health indicator computed from a threshold).

Add/extend backend canister query APIs in `backend/main.mo` to support the Dashboard “Equity Curve” chart with real data (no mock data), returning a time-series suitable for a monotone line chart (timestamps + equity value points) for the authenticated user.

Add/extend backend canister query APIs in `backend/main.mo` to support the Dashboard “Live Logs” audit feed with real data (no mock data), returning a paginated list of log rows containing at minimum: time, signal (buy/sell), asset (e.g., BTC/ETH or symbol), price, and trust score data needed to display “100% verified”.

Add a NEW `vault_backend` API in `backend/main.mo` to link a generated bot Ed25519 public key to the authenticated user’s Principal (sent from the Settings “Connect Bot” workflow), and ensure it updates the stored user profile data without breaking existing profile APIs used by the current frontend.
